channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Metallica Deliver Epic Set at London Stadium During 'No Repeats' Tour
item.description: Metallica delivered a career-spanning performance at the first of their two 'no repeats' shows at London Stadium, marking the conclusion of their UK and Ireland leg of the 'M72' world tour. The band, ...

item.title: Mexico's Kidnapping Crisis and the Challenge of Hosting the World Cup
item.description: As England prepares to face Mexico in the FIFA World Cup, a parallel crisis unfolds in the country's streets. Over 130,000 people have been forcibly disappeared since 2006, with cartel violence escala...

item.title: Proposed ATF rule could boost online gun sales and impact firearm regulations
item.description: The Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF) has proposed a rule change that would allow licensed dealers to ship firearms directly to in-state residents after online identity verific...

item.title: Teen Arrested for Allegedly Enticing Minors Online via Roblox
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has arrested a teenager in Pampanga over allegations of attempting to entice minors through the online platform Roblox. According to reports, the suspect is ...

item.title: Critics condemn Trump's impact on American patriotism during July 4 celebrations
item.description: The article reflects on the contrast between the patriotic spirit of the 1976 bicentennial and the current political climate, where former President Donald Trump has become inextricably linked with na...

item.title: Two Brothers Charged with Land Fraud by EFCC
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has charged two brothers with fraud for allegedly reselling the same piece of land to multiple buyers. The case stems from a petition filed by Mr. N...

item.title: July Fourth Fireworks Cause Hazardous Air Quality in Southern California
item.description: Fireworks celebrations on July 4th are expected to significantly degrade air quality across Southern California, with hazardous pollution levels anticipated from Saturday evening through Sunday aftern...

item.title: India's evolving career landscape: Beyond traditional paths
item.description: The article explores the shifting career aspirations in India, moving away from traditional fields like medicine and engineering toward diverse opportunities. It highlights factors such as economic pr...

item.title: TMC faces leadership crisis as Chandrima Bhattacharya resigns amid internal rebellion
item.description: Chandrima Bhattacharya, the newly appointed state president of the Trinamool Congress (TMC) in West Bengal, has resigned from her post just a month after taking charge. Her resignation comes amid grow...

item.title: Proposed ATF Rule Could Boost Online Gun Sales and Impact Industry Dynamics
item.description: The Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF) is proposing a significant change to US gun policy that would allow licensed dealers to ship firearms directly to in-state residents. This...

item.title: Study Links Food Preservatives to Higher Risks of Hypertension and Cardiovascular Disease
item.description: A large-scale study published in *Wired* links common food preservatives to increased risks of hypertension and cardiovascular disease. Researchers analyzed data from the NutriNet-Santé cohort study, ...

item.title: Prof Marianne Wanjiru Mureithi Breaks Barriers as Kenya's First Female Medical Microbiology Professor
item.description: Prof Marianne Wanjiru Mureithi has made history as Kenya's first female Professor of Medical Microbiology at the University of Nairobi. Her journey, spanning training in the UK, The Gambia, and MIT/Ha...

item.title: Rappler's July 2026 Corrections List
item.description: This article serves as Rappler's corrections page for July 2026, listing reports that have been revised or updated. The summary highlights the importance of reviewing the Editor’s Note in each correct...

item.title: Man with violent history faces potential release from Ontario hospital amid public safety concerns
item.description: Delroy Apple, a man with a documented history of violence and mental health struggles, is being considered for gradual release from an Ontario psychiatric facility. Despite his past crimes—including a...

item.title: Tanzania Bureau of Standards Seizes Hazardous Goods from Markets
item.description: The Tanzania Bureau of Standards (TBS) has intensified operations to combat substandard and unregistered products, destroying 5.6 tonnes of hazardous goods in Mkuranga District. Confiscated items incl...

item.title: FIA arrests individual impersonating NA employee using forged documents
item.description: The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) arrested Abdul Wajid, a Ghotki resident, for impersonating a National Assembly (NA) official using a forged appointment letter bearing the Speaker's forged signa...

item.title: ACB discovers benamidars linked to Eknath Shinde during raids
item.description: The article highlights the discovery of illegal properties (benamidars) belonging to Maharashtra's Chief Minister Eknath Shinde by the Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) during recent raids. This developmen...

item.title: Pauline Hanson Rules Out Moira Deeming, Trump's Call with Starmer, and Germany's Sickie Tradition
item.description: The article details political developments involving Australian political figures and international events. Pauline Hanson, leader of the One Nation party, has ruled out recruiting Victorian Liberal M...

item.title: Claire Castro condemns alleged harassment during Philippine community protest in Vancouver
item.description: Palace Press Officer Undersecretary Claire Castro spoke out about an alleged harassment incident she experienced in Vancouver, Canada, during President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr.’s official visit...

item.title: CAF Launches Bidding Process for Future AFCON Tournaments
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) has officially launched the bidding process for the 2028, 2032, and 2036 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) tournaments, inviting all 54 member associations to c...
